english: of an acrid taste, used as tea'; in Lh. Potentilla Salesovii, of which neither the one nor the other fact is known to me.

1. = nor (nor) wealth, riches, treasures, nor-dbyig (nor dbyig) id. Dzl.; dbyig-čan (dbyig can) rich, dbyig-med (dbyig med) poor Cs.; dbyig-maṅ (dbyig mang) Lex. - 2. prob. = dbyig-ynyén (dbyig gnyen), precious stone or a kind of such Glr. and elsewh.

1. syn. with kloṅ (klong), com. nám-mk'ai dbyiṅs (nam mkha'i dbyings) or dbyiṅs (dbyings) alone: the heavens, celestial region, rgyáb-la brag dmar nám-mk'ai dbyiṅs (rgyab la brag dmar nam mkha'i dbyings) red rocks behind and the expanse of heaven Mil.; kyeu dbyiṅ-su yal (khye'u dbyings su yal) the youth disappearing was carried up to heaven Pth.; dbyiṅs-na bźúgs-pai ḍá-ki-ma (dbyings na bzhugs pa'i da ki ma) Mil. - 2. height Schr.; the above passage was also rendered: red rocks behind, as high as heaven. - 3. in metaphysics an undefined idea of extent, region, space, धातु, (cf. kloṅ (klong)), čós-kyi dbyiṅs (chos kyi dbyings), धर्मधातु, not 'the wide diffusion of religion' Sch., but a mere fanciful notion, or as it is expressed Wts. 143: le monde intellectuel de Bouddha; of highly learned Lamas the words are used: tŭgs-dgóṅs čos-dbyiṅs-su tĭm (thugs dgongs chos dbyings su thim) C.; and also dbyiṅs (dbyings) alone: bló-yi byéd-pa dbyiṅs-su sbos (blo yi byed pa dbyings su sbos) Glr. hide your mental activity in the heavens, i.e. let it be reduced to nothing; so prob. also Tar. 38, 10, púṅ-po lhág-mo méd-pai dbyiṅs-su (phung po lhag mo med pa'i dbyings su), where nothing of the skandha is left remaining. Sch.: dbyiṅs-su (dbyings su) in a body, in one mass, whole, entire(?).
or ryin byéd-pa (ryin byed pa) Sch., to incite, instigate, set on.

shape, figure, form, byá-dbyibs-čan (bya dbyibs can) having the shape of a bird Lt.; śiṅ-rtai dbyibs daṅ ͦdra (shing rta'i dbyibs dang 'dra) shaped like a waggon or carriage Glr.; skyés-pai dbyibs-la ṅós-bzuṅ-ba (skyes pa'i dbyibs la ngos bzung ba) to learn the nature (of plants) from the shape in which they grow; *ă-mẹ yib dug* (a me yib dug) he quite resembles his mother in shape W.; dbyibs légs-pa (dbyibs legs pa) B. a fine figure, *sóg-po* (sog po) an ugly figure W., or also: of a handsome (or ugly) form; dbyibs zlúm-por yod (dbyibs zlum por yod) it has a round shape Glr.

1. small staff, wand, rod, e.g. used as a magic wand, sun-dial etc. Cs. - 2. Lex.: = ču-tśod (chu tshod) q.v.; Sch.: dbyúg-gu re-bźi (dbyug gu re bzhi), '64 equal parts of weight or measure; 64 quarters of an hour, or 16 hours'; but 64 ču-tśod (chu tshod) would make as much as 25 3/5 hours.

I. vb. pf. dbyugs (dbyugs) 1. to swing, brandish, flourish, a stick, a sword; to wag, rṅá-ma (rnga ma) the tail Cs; *yug yug jhẹ'-pa* (yug yug byed pa) W., to swing to and fro, to dangle; *yug toṅ* (yug thong) W., swing! dangle! - 2. to throw, cast, fling, *gyál-kar-nẹ do* (rgya skar nas rdo) C., to fling a stone through a window; to throw away, to throw down, *yúg-lẹ źog* (dbyug la zhog) C. (= *páṅ-te bor* (spangs te bor) W.), throw it away! - II. sbst, stick, C.; *yúg-pa gyáb-pa* (dbyug pa rgyab pa) C. to strike, to beat with a stick. dbyúg-to (dbyug to) Glr., dbyúg-ťo (dbyug tho), id. (Sch. club?) Lex.: = bér-ka (ber ka), दण्ड; dbyug-ťo-čan (dbyug tho can) wielding a stick; n.p.
fut., and in C. secondary form to the pres. ͦbyiṅ-pa ('bying pa).

(regular pronunciation *yé-wa* (ye wa), com. *'é-wa* ('e wa)). I. vb. fut., and in C. secondary form of ͦbyéd-pa ('byed pa). - II. sbst. 1. parting, partition, division, distinction, classification Thgy. - 2. section, part, class, species, dbyé-ba nyi-śu ysuṅs (dbye ba nyi shu gsungs) twenty different species are named Lt.; yi-ge ͦdi dbyé-ba ynyis (yi ge 'di dbye ba gnyis) these letters are divided into two classes; hence like sna-tsogs (sna tshogs): sgyu-rtsál dbyé-ba (sgyu rtsal dbye ba) manifold arts, artifices Smbh. - dbye-brál (dbye bral) Lex.: discord, dissension.

(*yén-pa* (yen pa), com. *'én-pa* ('en pa), = dbén-pa (dben pa)), difference, dissension, discord, schism, dge-ͦdún-gyi dbyén-pa byéd-pa (dge 'dun gyi dbyen pa byed pa) to create discord, to cause a schism among the priesthood Dzl.; dbyen ͦbyéd pa (dbyen 'byed pa) to make a difference, to discriminate Sch.

wylie: dbyer med, dbye ru med pa, dbyer mi phyed pa
